neighborhoods.name: Sundance Square / Downtown
neighborhoods.description: Pedestrian-friendly downtown with Sundance Square plaza, museums, and Western heritage entertainment

housing.predominantType: Loft apartments, condos, and renovated buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Fort Worth. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Fort Worth

neighborhoods.name: Southlake / Keller
neighborhoods.description: Affluent northern suburbs with Southlake Town Square, excellent schools, and upscale shopping

housing.predominantType: Large single-family homes in upscale neighborhoods

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Fort Worth's center and services.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Fort Worth

neighborhoods.name: Benbrook / White Settlement
neighborhoods.description: Western suburbs with lakeside recreation, affordable housing, and community character

housing.predominantType: Ranch houses, modest homes, and apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Fort Worth

neighborhoods.name: Burleson / Crowley
neighborhoods.description: Southern suburbs with family-oriented communities, good schools, and growing amenities
neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Fort Worth
